ddls:
CREATE TABLE "graph_update_testcases" (
  "testcase_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test case */,
  "graph_name" TEXT /* Name of the graph used in the test case */,
  "mean_time" REAL /* Mean time taken by the graph update operation */,
  "std_dev_time" REAL /* Standard deviation of time taken by the graph update operation */,
  "min_time" REAL /* Minimum time taken by the graph update operation */,
  "max_time" REAL /* Maximum time taken by the graph update operation */,
  "median_time" REAL /* Median time taken by the graph update operation */,
  "num_iterations" INTEGER /* Number of iterations performed in the test case */,
  "testcase_description" TEXT /* Description of the test case */,
  "testcase_date" TEXT /* Date when the test case was created */,
  "testcase_status" TEXT /* Status of the test case (e.g., active, completed, paused) */,
  "testcase_creator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the test case */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("testcase_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_graph_update_testcases_graph_name FOREIGN KEY ("graph_name") REFERENCES graphs ("graph_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "graphs" (
  "graph_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each graph */,
  "graph_name" TEXT /* Name of the graph */,
  "graph_type" TEXT /* Type of the graph (e.g., directed, undirected) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the graph */,
  "node_count" INTEGER /* Number of nodes in the graph */,
  "edge_count" INTEGER /* Number of edges in the graph */,
  "graph_creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the graph was created */,
  "graph_creator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the graph */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("graph_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "graph_update_algorithms" (
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each algorithm */,
  "algorithm_name" TEXT /* Name of the algorithm */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the algorithm */,
  "parameters" TEXT /* Parameters used by the algorithm */,
  "algorithm_creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the algorithm was created */,
  "algorithm_creator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the algorithm */,
  "algorithm_version" TEXT /* Version of the algorithm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("algorithm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "algorithm_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm */,
  "graph_id" INTEGER /* ID of the graph */,
  "mean_time" REAL /* Mean time taken by the algorithm on the graph */,
  "std_dev_time" REAL /* Standard deviation of time taken by the algorithm on the graph */,
  "min_time" REAL /* Minimum time taken by the algorithm on the graph */,
  "max_time" REAL /* Maximum time taken by the algorithm on the graph */,
  "median_time" REAL /* Median time taken by the algorithm on the graph */,
  "performance_date" TEXT /* Date when the performance metrics were recorded */,
  "performance_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the performance metrics */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_algorithm_performance_algorithm_id FOREIGN KEY ("algorithm_id") REFERENCES graph_update_algorithms ("algorithm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_algorithm_performance_graph_id FOREIGN KEY ("graph_id") REFERENCES graphs ("graph_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "testcase_algorithm_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "testcase_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testcase_algorithm_mapping_algorithm_id FOREIGN KEY ("algorithm_id") REFERENCES graph_update_algorithms ("algorithm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testcase_algorithm_mapping_testcase_id FOREIGN KEY ("testcase_id") REFERENCES graph_update_testcases ("testcase_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "testcase_graph_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "testcase_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  "graph_id" INTEGER /* ID of the graph */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testcase_graph_mapping_graph_id FOREIGN KEY ("graph_id") REFERENCES graphs ("graph_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testcase_graph_mapping_testcase_id FOREIGN KEY ("testcase_id") REFERENCES graph_update_testcases ("testcase_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, researcher, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "testcase_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_testcase_id FOREIGN KEY ("testcase_id") REFERENCES graph_update_testcases ("testcase_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "graph_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each graph type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the graph type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the graph type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "algorithm_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each algorithm type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the algorithm type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the algorithm type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "testcase_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each result */,
  "testcase_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the test case */,
  "result_date" TEXT /* Date when the result was obtained */,
  "result_details" TEXT /* Detailed information about the result */,
  "result_creator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who recorded the result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testcase_results_result_creator_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_creator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testcase_results_testcase_id FOREIGN KEY ("testcase_id") REFERENCES graph_update_testcases ("testcase_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "testcase_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "testcase_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "tag_creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the tag was created */,
  "tag_creator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testcase_tags_tag_creator_id FOREIGN KEY ("tag_creator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testcase_tags_testcase_id FOREIGN KEY ("testcase_id") REFERENCES graph_update_testcases ("testcase_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "graph_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "graph_id" INTEGER /* ID of the graph */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "tag_creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the tag was created */,
  "tag_creator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_graph_tags_graph_id FOREIGN KEY ("graph_id") REFERENCES graphs ("graph_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_graph_tags_tag_creator_id FOREIGN KEY ("tag_creator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "algorithm_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "tag_creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the tag was created */,
  "tag_creator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_algorithm_tags_algorithm_id FOREIGN KEY ("algorithm_id") REFERENCES graph_update_algorithms ("algorithm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_algorithm_tags_tag_creator_id FOREIGN KEY ("tag_creator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
